source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: ATP2B1 is expressed heterogeneously on human LT-HSC across ontogeny
ATP2B1 expression stratifies CD49f+ LT-HSC into functionally distinct subpopulations
CD49f+ATP2B1+ LT-HSC exhibit superior clonogenic potential, repopulation, and self-renewal
scMultiome profiling identifies TFEB-mediated endolysosomal programs in CD49f+ATP2B1+ HSC
ATP2B1 expression links to calcium homeostasis and lysosomal activity in LT-HSC
These findings provide a framework to dissect heterogeneous molecular programs in LT-HSC

typical_pattern: Opening statement of main finding with marker identification
Comparison to prior markers and functional validation
Ontogeny-specific expression patterns with quantitative detail
Mechanistic interpretation linking expression to pathways
Broader implications for understanding HSC biology
Explicit limitations section with technical caveats

likely_priorities: Functional validation across multiple HSC sources and assays
Single-cell resolution molecular profiling to support phenotypic claims
Mechanistic linkage between surface marker and stemness programs
Quantitative assessment of HSC purity and repopulation capacity
Explicit acknowledgment of technical limitations and alternative explanations
likely_attacks: Reliance on CB for gold-standard assays rather than testing all sources equally
Lack of direct genetic perturbation to prove ATP2B1 sufficiency for stemness
Use of intrafemoral injection only without testing homing capacity
Comparison between SGM3 and NSG mice not performed
Autophagy flux not directly measured despite mechanistic claims
In vitro culture artifacts when attempting genetic validation
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The Limitations section explicitly states: reliance on CB as representative; conclusions limited to intrafemoral injection; need for direct comparison between SGM3 and NSG mice; lack of determination whether ATP2B1 expression is sufficient; challenges with genetic perturbation due to culture-induced artifacts and transgene size; inability to isolate which transcript variants are important; lack of direct autophagy flux measurement. The discussion also hedges mechanistic claims with 'suggest', 'hypothesis', and 'should' when linking ATP2B1 to endolysosomal programs.

examples.quoted_phrase: we demonstrate that ATP2B1 is expressed on the surface of human LT-HSC
our data suggest its expression is reflective of functional stemness programs
scMultiome profiling identified TFEB-mediated control of catabolic processes as one of the molecular mechanisms underpinning the distinct stemness advantage
we hypothesize that the ATP2B1+CD49f+ HSC we have identified in humans are the ortholog of the aforementioned autophagy-activated, stress-resistant HSC
Our study provides a framework to dissect the heterogeneous molecular programs in LT-HSC

phrase_evidence.phrase: Here, we demonstrate that ATP2B1 is expressed on the surface of human LT-HSC
ATP2B1 surface staining identifies a single continuous HSC population rather than discrete subsets
When compared to CD49f+ATP2B1– HSC, the CD49f+ATP2B1+ subset had higher serial clonogenic progenitor replating and better repopulation and self-renewal
our data suggest its expression is reflective of functional stemness programs
interrogation of CD49f+ATP2B1+ HSC across ontogeny should reveal both common stemness programs
scMultiome profiling identified TFEB-mediated control of catabolic processes as one of the molecular mechanisms
These findings refine our prior studies
Our work solidifies the value of quantifying the nuclear-versus-non-nuclear localization of TFEB
Our study suggests a link between ATP2B1 surface expression and LT-HSC-specific endolysosomal programs
we hypothesize that the ATP2B1+CD49f+ HSC we have identified in humans are the ortholog
Future studies employing robust low-input-to-single-cell metabolomic profiling should shed considerable light
our findings serve as a foundation to accelerate a deeper understanding
Limitations of the Study
We relied on CB as being representative for the gold-standard xenotransplantation assays
our conclusions are limited to HSC transplanted by intrafemoral injection
a direct comparison between SGM3 and NSG mice needs to be done
we have not determined if the expression of ATP2B1 is sufficient to endow stemness properties
overexpressing ATP2B1 in primary cells is challenging owing to the larger size of the ATP2B1 transgene
future mechanistic studies are needed to investigate this hypothesis
Representative images and quantification of confocal microscopy images
CD49f+ATP2B1+ LT-HSC are enriched for TFEB-regulated lysosome-associated programs
CD49f+ATP2B1+ LT-HSC display superior in vivo repopulation capacity and self-renewal
demonstration of unit purity in transplantation using CB HSC
the latter finding is consistent with prior studies
pinpointing particular pathways regulating HSC functionality in the human setting
Despite our data suggesting that ATP2B1 expression and the endolysosomal pathways are connected
ATP2B1 immunophenotypic expression stratified human CD49f+ LT-HSC into functionally distinct subpopulations
CD49f+ATP2B1+ LT-HSC exhibited superior long-term repopulation and self-renewal capacities in vivo
Molecular profiling by scMultiome and immunofluorescence microscopy point to enrichment of an HSC self-renewal program
Our study provides a framework to dissect the heterogeneous molecular programs in LT-HSC

verification_status.notes: All phrases extracted are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers included. Claim strength profile reflects the paper's balanced use of hedges and assertions. Reviewer preference profile inferred directly from the explicit Limitations section and hedged mechanistic claims in the discussion.

_phrase_violations.phrase: Future studies employing robust low-input-to-single-cell metabolomic profiling should shed considerable light
Representative images and quantification of confocal microscopy images
ATP2B1 immunophenotypic expression stratified human CD49f+ LT-HSC into functionally distinct subpopulations
